summary: ISS-1399 (WIN-004 c4 H1, moderate, orphan-picked-up 2026-05-14, escalated to Opus deep-attack) is already-fixed. The c4 H1 preemptive_recommendation (LLSVP-asymmetry parenthetical with timing-independence framing + asymmetry-falsification finding clause) was implemented by decider-self-apply on 2026-05-05 via ISS-1856 (fixed_at 2026-05-05T21:22:00Z, verified_at 2026-05-05T23:05:46Z by verifier v1.0.0). C6 curmudgeon review (2026-05-09T08:22:21Z) independently verified both surgical edits landed verbatim, defense_survives dropped c4=4 → c5=3 → c6=2. Current data/wins.json:WIN-004.finding and .detail_verdict_text contain the c4 H1 preemption verbatim (live verification this run, clone @ 8045e29e). RECOMMEND: close ISS-1399 as already-fixed-by-related (ISS-1856), no patch required, cross-reference ISS-1856 in closure for audit trail. Out-of-scope observations flagged in EXP for tracker visibility: (a) c5/c6 hole #2 (detail_evidence "broader MHD models" unsourced) still surviving — separate ISS scope; (b) detail_extra null with PRED-013 feasibility math un-integrated across three cycles — separate concern.
